This is a blank template. When you run jrc_verify_attr … --report, a copy is generated automatically with all analysis results and the chart filled in. Open that generated file in Word (File → Open, then Save As .docx) to complete the highlighted fields, or print directly to PDF from a browser.

Design Verification Report

Statistical Tolerance Interval Analysis

Report ID[auto-generated on run]
Generated[auto-generated on run]
Scriptjrc_verify_attr v2.0 — JR Anchored
StatusDRAFT — complete all highlighted fields before use
1. Purpose and Scope
Requirement Reference [enter design input or design output requirement ID and description]
Design Input / Output [state whether this verifies a Design Input (DI) or Design Output (DO)]
Purpose of Verification [describe what is being verified and why the tolerance interval method was selected]
Acceptance Criterion [restate the acceptance criterion, e.g.: at least 95% of the population lies within spec, with 95% confidence]
2. Test Setup
Data File[auto-filled: path to input CSV]
Column / Characteristic[auto-filled: column name]
Sample Size (N)[auto-filled]
Proportion (P)[auto-filled]
Confidence (C)[auto-filled]
Spec Limit 1 (LSL)[auto-filled or —]
Spec Limit 2 (USL)[auto-filled or —]
Test Conditions [describe measurement conditions, equipment, operator, and date of measurements]
3. Statistical Method
Method Normal-theory statistical tolerance interval (K-factor method, EXACT)
Interval Type[auto-filled: 1-sided or 2-sided]
Transformation[auto-filled: none / normal / boxcox (λ=…)]
Reference Krishnamoorthy & Mathew (2009). Statistical Tolerance Regions. Wiley.
4. Results
N (valid observations)[auto-filled]
Mean[auto-filled]
SD[auto-filled]
K-factor[auto-filled]
TI Lower Limit[auto-filled or —]
TI Upper Limit[auto-filled or —]
Spec Limit 1 (LSL)[auto-filled or —]
Spec Limit 2 (USL)[auto-filled or —]
✅ Verification outcome: PASS  /  ❌ FAIL  — auto-filled
5. Verification Chart
Chart embedded here when generated by jrc_verify_attr —report
6. Conclusion
Outcome [auto-filled: PASS / FAIL]
Conclusion [state whether the design requirement is verified; summarise the statistical evidence]
Deviations / Observations [NONE — or describe any deviations from the planned test method]
7. Approvals
Role Name Signature Date
Performed by
Reviewed by
Approved by
Verification Report Template — jrc_verify_attr v2.0 — JR Anchored
